data.title: Helium Evolution appoints Malcolm Adams as president, CEO and director
data.description:
• Helium Evolution (HEVI:CA) has announced the appointment of Malcolm Adams as President, Chief Executive Officer and Director, effective February 1, 2026.
  • Mr. Adams brings over 30 years of experience in energy, private equity and technology sectors. Source: Press Release

data.title: Bob's Discount Furniture aims for a $2.5B valuation in its IPO
data.description:
Home furnishings retailer Bob's Discount Furniture (BOBS) indicated on Monday that it aims to raise as much as $370 million in its IPO in the U.S.

The Manchester, Connecticut-based company plans to offer 19.45M shares in an expected range of $17 to $19 per share. At the top end of the range, Bob's (BOBS) would be valued at $2.5B. Following the IPO, Bain-advised investment funds and affiliates will still own roughly three-quarters of the outstanding common stock.

Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS) traces its roots to the early 1990s when founder Bob Kaufman opened the first store in Newington, Connecticut, opportunistically taking over a bankrupt furniture location during a recession and negotiating a low–fixed-cost lease tied to sales. The brand grew through the 1990s and 2000s across New England by emphasizing a no-frills store environment, everyday low pricing, and heavy use of quirky TV and radio ads featuring Kaufman himself as the on-air pitchman.

Over time, the company expanded beyond its regional base, built out a distribution network, and moved its headquarters to Manchester, Connecticut. The value furniture chain now has over 200 locations.

Looking ahead, Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS) is pursuing an aggressive physical-store growth strategy while also positioning itself for omnichannel competition. Industry insiders said the IPO filing signals an intent to fund further growth plans.

Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS) competes in a broad and fragmented landscape that includes national furniture chains, big-box and department stores, and pure-play e-commerce players. Haverty Furniture Companies (HVT), Ashley HomeStore, Macy's (M), Kohl's (KSS), Wayfair (W), Bassett Furniture (BSET), and Rooms To Go are all considered competitors to some degree.

data.title: Century Aluminum, EGA to build first U.S. aluminum smelter since 1980
data.description:
Century Aluminum (CENX) and Emirates Global Aluminum announced plans Monday to build the first new primary aluminum production plant in the U.S. since 1980, in a joint venture to be owned 60% by EGA and 40% by Century.

The new plant, to be built in Inola, Oklahoma, is expected to produce 750K metric tons/year of aluminum, larger than previously planned and more than doubling current U.S. production.

The companies said the project will use EGA's latest state-of-the-art EX technology, the most advanced ever installed in the U.S.

Construction of the project is expected to start by year-end 2026, with production planned to begin by the end of the decade; detailed engineering work is already underway, and negotiations for a long-term power supply are progressing.

"Our partner EGA brings world-class smelting technology and construction expertise that are fast-tracking our collective efforts to realize President Trump’s vision of rapidly increasing domestic primary aluminum production," Century Aluminum (CENX) CEO Jesse Gary said.

data.title: Velocity plans $500M notes offering; posts preliminary 2025 results
data.description:
• Velocity Financial (VEL) on Monday announced that Velocity Commercial Capital, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Velocity, plans to offer up to $500 million of senior notes due 2031, in a private placement, subject to market and other conditions.
  • The company intends to use approximately $222.7 million of the net proceeds to redeem its outstanding 7.125% senior secured notes due 2027, with the remainder for general corporate purposes, including repayment of borrowings under warehouse and revolving loan facilities.

  • Velocity said up to $75 million of the proceeds could be used toward a potential business acquisition under consideration.

  • The notes will be offered to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to certain investors outside the United States under Regulation S and will not be registered under U.S. securities laws.
  • Separately, the company has reported preliminary unaudited results for the fourth quarter and full year that ended December 31, 2025.
  • For the fourth quarter, the company said it expects net income of at least $35 million, up at least 70% from a year earlier, with diluted earnings per share of at least $0.90. Total loan originations were about $635 million, while portfolio net interest margin was at least 3.55%, compared with 3.70% a year earlier.
  • For full-year 2025, Velocity said it expects net income of at least $105 million, up at least 53%, with diluted earnings per share of at least $2.75. Total loan originations were about $2.72 billion, while portfolio net interest margin was at least 3.57%, compared with 3.56% a year ago.
  • Consensus for FY25 and Q4 revenues are $187.33M and $52.54M.
  • The company said the total portfolio unpaid principal balance was about $6.49 billion at year-end, up about 28%, while nonperforming loans were between 8.5% and 8.6% of total loans, compared with 10.7% a year earlier.
  • VEL closed -1.32% at $19.39.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sarepta rises on three-year Elevidys data
data.description:
• Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT) is up ~6% in premarket trading Monday after releasing positive top-line three-year results for Elevidys (delandistrogene moxeparvovec) in Duchenne muscular dystrophy. 
  • Results from the phase 3 EMBARK study showed that Elevidys led to at least a 70% reduction in the rate of decline relative to the propensity-weighted external control group, based on Time to Rise (TTR) and 10-meter walk/run (10MWR).  
  • With a mean age of nine, patients receiving Elevidys achieved mean North Star Ambulatory Assessment (NSAA) scores above baseline three years after treatment.

data.title: Qualcomm-MediaTek competition heating up, Mizuho says
data.description:
• The competition between Qualcomm (QCOM) and MediaTek is heating up, investment firm Mizuho said.
  • “Our call noted growing competition between [Qualcomm and Mediatek] with QCOM entering more low/mid-end, while MTK remains aggressive on pricing in the high-end with D9400/9500, but QCOM still the leader,” Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh wrote in a note to clients. “We believe Samsung could potentially increase Exynos2600 deployments (QCOM noted at 75%+ penetration in Samsung) into 2026E with EU/KR. We believe the China RF supply chain continues to expand with Maxscend, Vanchip, and Hisilicon (via CETC, Runxin, SMEI) working to increase penetration into mid-end phones at CN OEMs.”
  • Rakesh reiterated his Neutral rating on Qualcomm, but lowered his price target to $160 from $175.

data.title: Packaging stocks are on watch on reports of containerboard price increase
data.description:
Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) hiked its containerboard price to $70 per ton, according to Jefferies. The price increase is set to go into effect on March 1.

Philip Ng highlighted that the firm expects the rest of the industry to match the increase in the coming weeks, especially if International Paper (IP) matches. "The timing of the announcement comes a little earlier than expected, with our contacts & RISI calling out an April window, but it's not shocking considering spring increases usually happen in early March or April," updated Ng.  

Notably, Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) has led the industry with a price hike announcement for January the past two years. Jefferies' view is that if the full $70 per ton increase is realized, major players such as International Paper (IP), Packaging Corporation of America (PKG), and Smurfit Westrock (SW) could see EBITDA jump by 10 to 20%.

As far as the overall containerboard market, checks by Jefferies indicate demand has been steady but seasonal, with demand picking up during agriculture season and the 9.4% of capacity closures starting to be felt.

data.title: NETSOL Technologies appoints Sardar Abubakr as finance chief
data.description:
• NETSOL Technologies (NTWK) has appointed Sardar Abubakr, FCCA, as Chief Financial Officer, effective January 20, 2026. 
  • Roger K. Almond, the company's current Chief Financial Officer, will remain with NETSOL as Chief Accounting Officer, responsible for NETSOL’s global accounting operations and the preparation of its Forms 10-Q and 10-K filings.
  • Most recently, he served as Vice President of New Business Ventures and M&A at Jazz, a subsidiary of VEON Group, where he led new venture creation, strategic transactions and major restructuring within a leading regional digital ecosystem. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Deutsche upgrades Cognizant to Buy ahead of Q4 earnings
data.description:
Deutsche Bank upgraded Cognizant Technology Solutions (CTSH) to Buy from Hold as they expect the IT services company to maintain durable growth above the industry average in the quarters ahead.

"The IT Services industry has been plagued by concurrent cyclical and secular headwinds over the past ~3 years, as the long-awaited pickup in demand has yet to materialize and GenAI has been an overhang on industry multiples," said Deutsche analysts, led by Nate Svensson, in a Monday investor note. "In this challenging environment where budgets remain tight, CTSH has positioned itself as a clear 'winner' from the shift towards vendor consolidation and cost transformation, which has manifested in solid and improving revenue growth."

Deutsche also increased its price target to $100 from $70.

"Under the leadership of CEO Ravi Kumar, the company has shifted its business model to focus on large deals of $100m+ in total contract value, which, although it took some time, has led to improving organic growth rates," Svensson added. "We attribute these share gains to the company's go-to-market strategy emphasizing actively proposing vendor consolidation, having a broad portfolio of services, and executing better than peers on leveraging GenAI (the company's '3 Vector' strategy)."

Cognizant plans to release its fourth quarter 2025 financial results pre-market on Wednesday, Feb. 4. A consensus estimate expects Cognizant to deliver revenue of $5.31B and earnings per share of $1.32.

data.title: AmpliTech group prices $9M unit offering
data.description:
• AmpliTech Group (AMPG) has offered 2.23M units at $4.055 per unit, in a registered direct offering for gross proceeds of ~$9.042M. Each unit consisted of one share of common stock, one Series A right to purchase one share of common stock at $5.00, and one Series B right to purchase one share of common stock at $6.00.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 27, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release
